reasoning:
The baseline value for 2025-06-22 is exactly 577,189 (the value reported for June 18). This value is already somewhat elevated compared to mid-month baselines typical in Q2 2025 (e.g., ~$450-$530B), due to early staging for the June quarter-end. 
- For June 29: This assesses the data for June 25, which represents quarter-end (Q2) window dressing. Historically, RRP spikes significantly during the final week of a quarter. Thus, an increase from 577k is very highly probable.
- For July 22: This measures mid-July data (likely July 16), after the quarter-end rollover. Without quarter-end effects, the value should revert to the structural baseline. In recent months, baseline non-end-of-month levels reside deep below 577k, around 510-530k. Therefore this is very likely to represent a decrease.
- For Sep 20: This assesses mid/late September. RRP may see some early creeping up for Q3-end, but 3 additional months of Federal Reserve quantitative tightening (QT) will drain system liquidity further, meaning the comparable run-up phase should logically be lower than June's 577k benchmark. 
- For Dec 19: Measuring mid-December. The overarching continuation of QT pushes probability further into negative territory across long time horizons, though year-end reporting factors and potential rate changes increase overall unertainty. The probabilities are constrained progressively back to 0.5 as required to reflect increasing forecast horizon uncertainty.
